questions.question: Your de Havilland Tiger Moth is currently at a CG of 78 inches and weighing 1,375 pounds, but you decide to move from the front seat to the rear seat. You weigh 192 pounds, and the rear seat is located 91 cm further back than the front seat. What is your new CG after you move?

You absolutely love shrimp so you purchase a rather large cooler which weighs 92.8 pounds to fill with shrimp. You add three 20-pound bags of ice and a whopping 293 pounds of shrimp. Once loaded into your airplane, which now weighs 2,450 pounds, you realize you are 3 inches aft of your CG limit. How many inches forward must you move the cooler?

Reference the previous question. As everyone is loading into your plane there is another problem. Fred was in the back but says he would get airsick unless he is in the front seat. Fred (172 LBS) will trade places with Betty (117 LBS) and moves from the back seat (45") to the front seat (12"). What is the final CG?
questions.context: Referenced question 25: You calculate your weight and balance prior to departure and come up with a gross weight of 2,435 LBS and CG of 18.9". Just prior to departure, Betty has an extra bag to get added. The new bag weighs 33 LBS and will be in the baggage compartment at 67". What is the new CG?

questions.question: You loaded up your plane for a short sightseeing flight for the eclipse last month. Unfortunately, you were slightly aft of your CG limit. If you switch the 240-pound rear seat (station 115) passenger with the 185-pound front seat (station 72) passenger, how much will your CG shift if your aircraft weighs 3,100 pounds?
You now decide to move 30 lbs from the baggage area, 75", to the back seat, 47". What will be the new CG?
questions.context: Referenced question 5: You add 36 gallons of Avgas into the fuel tank at an arm of 23". If the aircraft weighed 2,167 lbs and had a CG of 46.57" before refueling, what will the CG be after fueling?

questions.question: Your black lab Piper (92 lbs.) is flying with you today in your C172 (2,162 lbs) and he keeps jumping between the front seat and the back seat. How much will this shift your CG if your front seat is at 37 inches and your back seat is at 73 inches?

You have loaded up your plane with 4 passengers and are right at the max weight of 3,115 pounds. You weigh 170 pounds and will be the PIC for today's flight. You have Bob in the front seat (arm 37") with you and he weighs 225 pounds. In the back (arm 118"), you have Sally (115 pounds) and Jane (155 pounds). Your CG is a bit too far forward, 3 inches beyond the limit. What must you do to bring the CG within limits?

choices.text: It just won't work without ballast.

questions.correctChoice: It just won't work without ballast.

questions.question: You calculate the weight and balance of your C-172SP with yourself (157 lbs.) and your friend Annie (133 lbs.) in the front seats (arm 37 inches). Sebastian, your little pony at 274 lbs., is in the baggage compartment (arm 95 inches). You have a gross weight of 2378 lbs with a CG of 46.7 inches. Sebastian is a little cramped in the baggage compartment, so you move him to the back seat (arm 73 inches). How does the CG change?

Your aircraft (177,000 lbs, CG 147.3") transfers 4,845 liters of Jet A (6.8 lbs/gal) from the aft auxiliary tank (180") to the main tanks (155") in 7 minutes. Meanwhile, your engines are burning 6,300 lbs per hour total. After the transfer is complete, what is your new CG?

An aircraft is currently loaded up to 2,345 lbs with a CG of 43.2". Your friend (78 kg) decides that he would like to move to the back seat to sit with his Poodle (3 kg) for the next leg of the trip. How much will the CG shift when he moves from the front seat (Arm = 24") to the back seat (Arm = 45")?
For the next leg your two friends will switch seats. If your friend that was in the front (24") weighs 165 LBS what is the CG after they switch?
questions.context: Referenced question 11: You are planning to fly a couple friends on a weekend trip and have calculated your weight at 2175 LBS and CG of 15.7". At the last minute another friend decides to join. They weigh 145 LBS and will sit in the rear seat 55". What is the new CG?

questions.question: You're 2 inches out of CG. How much weight do you need to move to station 110 from station 50 to get back into CG? Total aircraft weight is 6850 LBS.
To adjust the CG after the change you decide to move 15 LBS of gear from the baggage area at 100" to under the front seat at 15". What is your final CG location?
questions.context: Referenced question 10: You have decided to upgrade your old aluminum prop to a new composite one! The old prop weighed 62 LBS while the composite model weights 41 LBS. Prior to the swap your aircraft weighed 1475 LBS with a CG of 11.63". If the prop is at -61" what will the new CG fall after the swap?

questions.question: Referencing the previous question, if your front seat passenger (168 LBS at 34") then decides they want to sit in the back seat (62") what will your CG be after they have moved?
questions.context: Referenced question 26: Your fully loaded aircraft weighs 2,160 LBS with a CG of 48". If you then load 35 US gallons of fuel at an average station of 36" what will the resultant aircraft CG be?

questions.question: You load your airplane for a short flight and find that your CG is 0.32" aft of the limit. You have filled all 4 seats as follows: The two front seats are occupied by 2 adults weighing 160 pounds each, at arm 44. The rear two seats are occupied by 2 adults weighing 180 pounds each, at arm 74. If your current gross weight is 2,844 pounds, which of the following choices will bring your CG to the aft limit of 66.5"?

choices.text: Switching 1 passenger from the front to the back
Removing 25 pounds from the baggage area at station 23
Adding 9.2 gallons of AVGAS at station 50
Adding 5 pound to the baggage area at station 85

questions.correctChoice: Adding 9.2 gallons of AVGAS at station 50

questions.question: Referencing the previous question, during flight the dog manages to escape and jumps up into the front seat at 36.5". Where will this put the CG?
questions.context: Referenced question 28: You have completed your planned weight and balance and determined your aircraft should weigh 2,570 LBS with a CG of 43.6". When one of your passengers arrives they inform you their dog has to come on the trip. Fine, but it's going in the back! What will be your new CG if it only weighs 15 LBS and will be located at 87"?

questions.question: If your front seat passenger (168 lbs at 34") then decides they want to sit in the back seat (62") what will be your CG after they have moved?
questions.context: Referenced question 5: Your fully loaded aircraft weighs 2,160 lbs with a CG of 48". If you then load 35 US gallons of fuel at an average station of 36", what will the resultant aircraft CG be?

questions.question: You depart your home airport at a weight of 3,284 pounds and a CG of 55.28" on a flight. The flight will take you 533 NM and your ground speed will be 173 knots. If you burn fuel at the rate of 18.6 gph from station 83.1", how much will your CG shift when you land?

Your airplane is loaded up to 3690 lbs with a CG of 47.8". The aft limit is 47", so you want to add ballast to the nose compartment until the airplane is within limits. You have some gallon-sized jugs of water in your car, and a quick Google search tells you that the density of water is 0.065 lbs/ounce (liquid). How many gallons do you add to the nose compartment at a station of -22.6"?

Your airplane has a current ramp weight of 2089 lbs. and a CG of 44.9 inches. You are trying to load it to the aft CG limit for maximum aerodynamic efficiency. How much ballast should you add to the aft baggage compartment (sta. 95") to get the airplane to the aft CG limit of 47.3"?

Using the information from the previous question, the new CG puts you 0.91" behind the aft CG limit of your aircraft. How much weight must you move from the baggage compartment at 160" to the nose compartment at 26" to bring the CG 1" inside the aft limit?
questions.context: Referenced question 32: Your aircraft is loaded to 2,480 lbs with a CG of 76.8". What will your new CG be if your 180 lb friend decides to join in the back seat at 108"?

questions.question: You are flying along in cruise when your friend decides he needs to use the bathroom. Good thing you decided to get the lavatory option, but he is going to have to dump it at your destination. If he weighs 190 lbs and was in the front seat at 63" what will be the CG when he walks back to the lavatory at 240"? The aircraft weighs 8,700 lbs and had a CG of 110" before he moved.
An airplane is currently loaded to its max allowed gross weight of 2500 pounds, but its CG is aft of the allowed limit by 1 inch. Which of these solutions will bring the airplane within limits?

Your airplane exceeds the manufacturer's weight and balance limitations. The CG is too far forward by 3 IN. You can readily move your cargo from station 50 to station 125, but how much of it will you need to move to reach the CG limit? The total gross weight is 2500 LBS. The cargo is canola oil in quart jars, with the total weight of the oil and jars being an average of 8 LBS per gallon.
(Use the above information for this question) You decide having the dry ice in the cabin isn't the best idea so you move it to the nose baggage area (station 30). What will be your new CG?
questions.context: Referenced question 16: You have your aircraft all loaded (4,630 LBS, CG of 76.3") when your friend shows up with a 35 LBS box of fish. If you put it in the aft baggage (station 150) what will be your new CG?

questions.question: To meet center of gravity limitations, you must move passengers from Zone A (located at an arm of 24') to Zone C (located at an arm of 76'). The C.G. must be shifted 1' aft. If each passenger weighs an average of 88.6 KG and the aircraft weighs 154,500 LBS how many passengers must be moved to get the C.G. within limits?
After you shifted 143 lbs. of your 3460 lb. airplane, your CG shifted from 47.6" to 46.7". From what station did you shift the weight if it is now at station 45.4"?

You are taking your friends for a $100 hamburger. Your aircraft, fully loaded, weighs 3844 lbs. with a moment of 323,000. On the way home, your 163 lb. friend decides he wants to let your other 221 lb. friend sit up front this time. If the front and rear seats are 39 inches apart, what is your new CG?
From the previous question, if your friend convinces your front seat passenger (150 LBS at station 75) to change places with him, what will be the new CG?
questions.context: Referenced question 8: As everyone is loading up in your airplane another friend decides they want to come along. You had planned to have a gross weight of 2436 LBS and a CG of 63.2". Your friend weighs 182 LBS and will sit in the back at station 116. What is your new CG?

questions.question: After completing your weight and balance, you find that your gross weight is 910 kg., and you are 1.5" aft of your center of gravity. How many 20 lb. boxes of cargo will you have to move to the next cargo hold 20" forward to come within limits?

You have the plane loaded to a gross weight of 4,260 lbs when your friends decide they want to change places. If Hank (190 lbs) moves to the back seat at station 73 and George (148 lbs) joins you up front at station 45 what will be your new CG assuming it was at 76.38"?

After calculating the weight and balance for a cross-country flight, your passengers decide to swap seats. Bill weighs 189 pounds and was sitting in the back seat (station 85.2). Alex was originally sitting in the front seat (station 49.1). Completely fueled, your aircraft weighs 810.45 kg. The original CG was 91.6. What is the new CG? Alex weighs 137 pounds.

You have loaded your aircraft to weigh 3319 lbs. The problem is that your CG of 68.7 inches is 1.1 inches forward of the limit. How many pounds need to be moved from the current CG to station 110.3 to bring you back within limits?

choices.text: 93 lbs. 2014 2620 TAS 137 mph; TC 165°; Deviation +3-; Cruise alt. 3500'; MH 179°

questions.question: How much weight do you have to move from the front baggage area at station 85" to the aft baggage area at station 210" to move the CG to the forward limit? Your aircraft weighs 8,400 pounds.
questions.context: Referenced question 32: The approved CG range for your aircraft is from 22.5% Mean Aerodynamic Chord (MAC) to 34.0% MAC. If the leading edge of MAC is located at station 100" (equivalent to 0% MAC) and the trailing edge is located at station 140" (100% MAC), what station is the leading edge of the approved MAC envelope? [[DIVIDER]] Referencing the previous question, you have completed all your weight and balance paperwork and determine that your CG is at 22.0% MAC.
